text: The Web server shall replicate content on-demand from the origin server.
The Web server shall initiate a request to trigger peering in the event of a Web hotspot.
The Web services host shall deliver content to end-users based on negotiated policies.
The policy agent shall determine which resources are delegatable.
The SLA-allocator shall perform provisioning and reservation of Web server resources including CPU, bandwidth, and storage.
The SLA-allocator shall enforce the terms of local and delegated SLAs.
The Mediator shall generate service requirements as the basis for negotiated relationships.
The Mediator shall pass service requirements to the Peering Agent.
The Mediator shall discover external resources in conjunction with the local Peering Agent.
The Mediator shall control the redirection of end-user requests to peered Web servers.
The Service Registry shall encapsulate resource and service information for each CDN.
The Service Registry shall enable Web servers to register and publish resource, service, and policy details.
The Service Registry shall supply local resource information to the Mediator during traffic surges.
The Policy Repository shall virtualize all policies within a peering arrangement including Web server-specific, mediator, and peering policies.
The Policy Repository shall provide rules to the Mediator for managing resource access.
The Peering Agent shall exchange policy, resource information, and service requirements with external Peering Agents.
The system shall perform load distribution by disseminating aggregated load information between CDN gateways.
The system shall perform request assignment and redirection at the DNS and gateway levels.
The system shall detect and reject malicious requests.
The system shall support bi-directional message passing over IP networks using TCP/IP.
The system shall use HTTP or FTP for interaction among surrogates.
The system shall communicate load information using distributed load indices such as Distributed Hash Tables.
